evidence.scientific_clarity: Ridgeline KDEs for all 6 clusters are clearly stacked with visible overlap, progressive rightward shift is readable, fulfilling the brief's claim.
evidence.typography: PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type 3 (bitmap-style subset); script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams, so text is not editable/selectable vector type.
evidence.palette: Six distinct Tableau-derived colors used categorically (no directional green/red misuse); palette is largely colorblind-safe but blue/teal pair (#4e79a7/#76b7b2) may be hard to distinguish for deuteranopes.
evidence.layout_density: Overlap=0.55 gives compact stacking with no gaping whitespace; left-side cluster labels are well-placed; x-axis range is tight around data.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and includes specific numerical claims; no legend needed (labels on ridges); minor issue: no explicit bandwidth citation in figure itself, only in caption.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF font is Type 3 (BMQQDV+DejaVuSans, Custom encoding, not Type 1/TrueType); fonttype=42 not set in script, so text is not selectable/editable in Illustrator.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial (PDF 48KB, PNG 48KB, SVG 332KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the plot type, kernel and bandwidth, describes the progressive shift with specific numerical anchors (Cluster 1 ~2.0, Cluster 3 ~3.1), and notes Cluster 6's heavy right tail.

overall_strengths: Clean ridgeline layout with well-chosen overlap and per-ridge white masking produces a publication-quality stacked KDE display.
Caption is detailed and quantitative, citing bandwidth, cluster-level medians, and directional trend — ready for submission.
overall_weaknesses: Font embedding uses Type 3 (DejaVu Sans bitmap subset) instead of Type 1/TrueType via pdf.fonttype=42, making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector editors.
Color palette has a blue/teal adjacency (#4e79a7 vs #76b7b2) that may be indistinguishable under deuteranopia; a perceptually uniform sequential or Okabe-Ito palette would be safer.
